data.output:
370		progressCh := make(chan agent.ProgressEvent, 64)
371		handle := &workerHandle{
372			record:       record,
373			cancel:       cancel,
374			logBuf:       logBuf,
375			logFile:      logFile,
376			progressCh:   progressCh,
377			progressDone: make(chan struct{}),
378			lastPhaseTS:  time.Now().UTC(),
379		}
380	
381		m.mu.Lock()
382		m.workers[id] = handle
383		m.mu.Unlock()
384	
385		m.ensureWatchdog()
386	
387		go m.drainProgress(id, handle, progressCh)
388		go m.runWorker(ctx, id, dir, spec, effectiveRoot, handle, multiLog, eventsFile, progressCh)
389	
390		return record, nil
391	}
392	
393	func (m *WorkerManager) StartPluginAction(spec PluginActionWorkerSpec, run PluginActionExecutor) (WorkerRecord, error) {
394		if run == nil {
395			return WorkerRecord{}, fmt.Errorf("plugin action executor is required")
396		}
397	
398		effectiveRoot := spec.ProjectRoot
399		if effectiveRoot == "" {
400			effectiveRoot = m.projectRoot
401		}
402	
403		if err := os.MkdirAll(m.rootDir, 0o755); err != nil {
404			return WorkerRecord{}, err
405		}
406	
407		id := "worker-" + time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405") + "-" + randomSuffix(4)
408		dir := filepath.Join(m.rootDir, id)
409		if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); err != nil {
410			return WorkerRecord{}, err
411		}
412	
413		spec.ProjectRoot = effectiveRoot
414		specData, _ := json.MarshalIndent(spec, "", "  ")
415		_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "spec.json"), append(specData, '\n'), 0o644)
416	
417		logPath := filepath.Join(dir, "worker.log")
418		logFile, err := os.OpenFile(logPath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_TRUNC, 0o644)
419		if err != nil {
420			return WorkerRecord{}, err
421		}
422	
423		startedAt := time.Now().UTC()
424		record := WorkerRecord{
425			ID:          id,
426			Kind:        "plugin-dispatch",
427			State:       "running",
428			Status:      "running",
429			ProjectRoot: effectiveRoot,
430			StdoutPath:  relToProject(m.projectRoot, logPath),
431			SpecPath:    relToProject(m.projectRoot, filepath.Join(dir, "spec.json")),
432			StartedAt:   startedAt,
433			Lifecycle: []WorkerLifecycleEvent{{
434				Action:    "start",
435				Actor:     "local-operator",
436				Timestamp: startedAt,
437				Detail:    fmt.Sprintf("%s plugin %s (%s)", spec.Action, spec.Name, spec.Scope),
438			}},
439		}
440		_ = m.writeRecord(dir, record)
441	
442		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
443		logBuf := &bytes.Buffer{}
444		multiLog := io.MultiWriter(logBuf, logFile)
445		progressCh := make(chan agent.ProgressEvent, 16)
446		handle := &workerHandle{
447			record:       record,
448			cancel:       cancel,
449			logBuf:       logBuf,
450			logFile:      logFile,
451			progressCh:   progressCh,
452			progressDone: make(chan struct{}),
453			lastPhaseTS:  startedAt,
454		}
455	
456		m.mu.Lock()
457		m.workers[id] = handle
458		m.mu.Unlock()
459	
460		m.ensureWatchdog()
461	
462		go m.drainProgress(id, handle, progressCh)
463		go m.runPluginAction(ctx, id, dir, spec, handle, multiLog, progressCh, run)
464	
465		return record, nil
466	}
467	
468	func (m *WorkerManager) runPluginAction(ctx context.Context, id, dir string, spec PluginActionWorkerSpec, handle *workerHandle, log io.Writer, progressCh chan agent.ProgressEvent, run PluginActionExecutor) {
469		startedAt := time.Now().UTC()
470		phaseSeq := 1
471		sendProgress(progressCh, agent.ProgressEvent{
472			EventID:   "evt-" + randomSuffix(8),
473			AttemptID: id,
474			WorkerID:  id,
475			ProjectID: spec.ProjectRoot,
476			Phase:     "running",
477			PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
478			TS:        startedAt,
479			Message:   fmt.Sprintf("%s plugin %s", spec.Action, spec.Name),
480		})
481		if log != nil {
482			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(log, "%s plugin %s (%s)\n", spec.Action, spec.Name, spec.Scope)
483		}
484	
485		state, err := run(ctx)
486		if ctxErr := ctx.Err(); ctxErr != nil && err == nil {
487			err = ctxErr
488		}
489	
490		phase := "done"
491		message := state
492		if err != nil {
493			phase = "failed"
494			message = err.Error()
495		}
496		phaseSeq++
497		sendProgress(progressCh, agent.ProgressEvent{
498			EventID:   "evt-" + randomSuffix(8),
499			AttemptID: id,
500			WorkerID:  id,
501			ProjectID: spec.ProjectRoot,
502			Phase:     phase,
503			PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
504			TS:        time.Now().UTC(),
505			ElapsedMS: time.Since(startedAt).Milliseconds(),
506			Message:   message,
507		})
508	
509		if log != nil {
510			if err != nil {
511				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(log, "failed: %s\n", err)
512			} else {
513				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(log, "completed: %s\n", state)
514			}
515		}
516	
517		close(progressCh)
518		<-handle.progressDone
519

- Resolver wires async worker in `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:129`

input.content:
No changes required.

The bead's acceptance criteria are already fully satisfied by code present at
base revision 83b98c1c3e1c43e2e131635eef19f0861760439c (the execute-bead
starting point for this attempt). The bead was re-queued with stale REVIEW:BLOCK
findings from a prior attempt, but those findings have already been addressed
by commits that are part of base:

  - 7133371f fix: run plugin dispatches as workers [ddx-7de0ce80]
  - efd5bacb fix: refresh plugin cards after dispatch [ddx-7de0ce80]
  - 92e12099 fix(plugins): skip plugin refresh on worker failure phases [ddx-7de0ce80]

Mapping from AC items to implementation at base:

AC1 (per-card in-flight tracking):
  cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/plugins/+page.svelte
  lines 56-60: dispatchingPlugins Set, inFlightWorkers Map<name,workerId>,
  inFlightActions, dispatchSnapshots, workerFailures.
  lines 115-117: isBusy(name) gate.
  lines 458-477: Install/Update buttons disabled while busy.

AC2 (worker-driven refresh):
  +page.svelte lines 225-238 (subscribeToWorker), 240-258 (handleWorkerTerminal).
  On non-failure terminal phase, refreshPlugins() runs, then clearInFlight(name).

AC3 (fallback polling):
  +page.svelte lines 190-223. scheduleFallbackPolling sets a 30s timer
  (configurable via __ddxPluginFallbackDelayMs). When it fires without a
  terminal event, startFallbackPolling polls every 2s until the snapshot
  changes. Playwright test at e2e/plugins.spec.ts:349 ("plugin update falls
  back to polling when worker stream drops terminal event") exercises this.

AC4 (in-flight visual state):
  +page.svelte lines 377-383. Loader2 spinner + "Installing..."/"Updating..."
  label replaces the status badge while workerId && action are set. Button
  disabled with Tooltip pointing to worker.

AC5 (failure surfacing):
  +page.svelte lines 392-405. workerFailures Map stores {workerId, action}
  on failure phases; renders red AlertCircle badge with link to worker page
  and title "Update failed"/"Install failed". handleWorkerTerminal (line 247)
  returns early on failure phases without calling refreshPlugins, preserving
  pre-dispatch state. dispatchError remains for synchronous mutation errors
  only (lines 287-289, 338-343).

AC6 (concurrent dispatch):
  Map-keyed tracking allows independent state per plugin. Header at
  +page.svelte lines 318-335 iterates inFlightWorkers.entries() and renders
  one link per active worker. Playwright test at e2e/plugins.spec.ts:381
  ("two plugin updates track separate workers concurrently").

AC7 (no extra refetch overhead):
  refreshPlugins (+page.svelte:184-188) calls only PLUGINS_LIST_QUERY, not
  the full page data loader.

AC8 (Playwright e2e):
  cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/plugins.spec.ts has dedicated tests
  covering in-flight state (line 284 "plugin update refreshes card when
  worker completes successfully"), failure (line 314), polling fallback
  (line 349), concurrent (line 381). Uses page.routeWebSocket to simulate
  the workerProgress subscription.

AC9 (no regressions):
  Install flow through ConfirmDialog uses the same dispatchPlugin() code
  path (lines 295-301), so install also gets in-flight tracking.
  Existing install test at e2e/plugins.spec.ts:199 (US-098.b) still asserts
  the Install flow.

Backend wiring:
  cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:119-145 — adapter uses
  WorkerManager.StartPluginAction with an executor callback.
  cli/internal/server/workers.go:393-519 (StartPluginAction + runPluginAction)
  — emits a "running" event at start and a "done" or "failed" phase event
  when DispatchPluginAction returns. Verified by
  TestWorkerManagerStartPluginActionPublishesTerminalProgress and
  TestWorkerManagerStartPluginActionPublishesFailureProgress
  (cli/internal/server/workers_test.go:47,93). Both pass locally.

Verification run in this worktree:
  - `go build ./...` in cli/: clean.
  - `go test ./internal/server/ -run 'Plugin' -v`: both worker tests PASS.

Because no code change is needed, committing a no-op would violate the
execute-bead instruction "Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code."
This rationale records the analysis so a reviewer can confirm and close the
bead, or reopen with a more specific gap to address.

data.text: No code changes made. The bead's AC is already satisfied at base `83b98c1c` by three prior commits (`7133371f`, `efd5bacb`, `92e12099`). The REVIEW:BLOCK findings in the bead notes were from earlier attempts and are resolved in base. Rationale written to `.ddx/executions/20260424T025656-af9dc9b3/no_changes_rationale.txt` with an AC→code mapping and local test verification (`go test ./internal/server/ -run Plugin` passes).
